project: Bike Garage - Phase 2.3 UI Polish and Cycling Identity
overview: A visual polish pass to make the app sleeker, more cohesive, and cycling-themed. Fixes dark text contrast, gives the bike photo and pin overlay more visual prominence, introduces a compact collapsible parts list, improves the price summary card styling, adds minimal scrollbars, and introduces cycling-themed loading animations and nomenclature throughout.
goals: Fix text contrast — replace #213547 dark text with a color that works on dark backgrounds
Make the bike photo + pin overlay the hero element of the results page
Introduce a compact, collapsible parts list to reduce visual overwhelm
Make the maintenance summary/price card more visually striking
Apply cycling-themed nomenclature and a cohesive visual identity
Add a cycling-themed loading animation for the analysis and pricing wait states
nonGoals: No changes to backend, API, or data model
No new features or functionality — polish only
No animation library installation — use CSS animations only unless a GIF is used for the loader
successMetrics: Text is legible on all backgrounds at a glance
Bike photo is visually the dominant element on the results page
Parts list is scannable in compact mode and expandable for detail
Loading state shows a cycling animation instead of a generic spinner
Page feels cohesive and purpose-built for cyclists, not a generic web app
openQuestions: Should the cycling loader be a CSS animation (spinning wheel/gear) or an embedded GIF?
Should cycling nomenclature extend to the upload page CTA (e.g. 'Start Your Inspection' instead of 'Analyze Bike')?

stack.notes: CSS-only animations preferred. No new npm packages unless absolutely necessary.

routes.name: Upload / Inspection Start
routes.purpose: Entry point — gets cycling-themed copy and loader

routes.name: Bike Inspection Report
routes.purpose: Primary page for all visual polish work

uiNotes: CONTRAST FIX: Replace all instances of color #213547 with #1a1a2e or a similarly deep navy that reads clearly on both white and light-gray backgrounds. Target: all body text, labels, table content.

PHOTO HERO: On desktop, increase the left column (photo + overlays) to 55% width (from ~50%). Add a subtle drop shadow and rounded corners to the photo container. The photo should feel like the centerpiece of the page.
PIN MARKERS: Ensure part pins on the photo are always visible (small colored dot at bounding box center) with a subtle pulse animation on hover. Pin color maps to priority level.

COMPACT PARTS LIST: Replace the expanded table rows with a compact single-line row by default showing: part name, condition badge, priority badge, repair action badge only. Each row has a chevron toggle to expand full detail (condition notes, repair notes, pricing). All rows collapsed by default. A 'Expand All / Collapse All' toggle at the section header.

PRICE SUMMARY CARD: Give the maintenance summary card a gradient background (subtle, e.g. deep navy to dark teal), white text, larger stat numbers (2rem), and a bottom border accent in the brand color. Add a bicycle icon (Unicode 🚲 or inline SVG) next to the title.
SCROLLBARS: Apply custom minimal scrollbars globally — 4px width, rounded, color #646cff on hover, transparent track. Use ::-webkit-scrollbar CSS.

CYCLING NOMENCLATURE: Rename UI labels to cycling-appropriate terms: 'Upload Photo' → 'Start Inspection', 'Analyze Bike' → 'Run Diagnosis', 'Parts Analysis' → 'Component Report', 'Overall Condition' → 'Bike Health', 'Repair Action' → 'Mechanic Recommendation', 'No results found' → 'No parts found in the peloton' (playful), loading text → 'Spinning up your diagnosis…'

CYCLING LOADER: Replace the generic spinner on the upload page and reanalysis overlay with a cycling-themed CSS animation. Options: a spinning bicycle wheel (SVG with rotating spokes), a gear spinning, or a small cyclist SVG animation. Must be CSS-only (keyframes). Show 'Spinning up your diagnosis…' below the animation.
TYPOGRAPHY: Increase base font size to 15px. Section headers use a slightly bolder weight (600). Part names in the compact list use font-weight 500.

COHESION: Add a thin top accent bar to the page (4px, gradient from --priority-5 blue to --condition-good teal) to give the app a branded feel. App title in the nav/header should read 'Bike Garage' in a bold, clean typeface.
rules: No hardcoded colors — all colors must reference existing CSS custom properties or new tokens added to :root in index.css
Compact row must show all three badges (condition, priority, repair action) on one line without wrapping at 1280px
Expanded row detail must show everything the current full row shows — no information loss
Cycling loader animation must loop infinitely and be dismissible (replaced by content on load)
All renamed labels must remain functionally identical — rename only, no logic changes
Scrollbar styling uses ::-webkit-scrollbar only — no JS scrollbar libraries

stories.title: Fix text contrast — replace dark navy text color with legible alternative
stories.description: As a user, I want all body text to be clearly legible against page backgrounds so the app is comfortable to read.
acceptanceCriteria: Replace all instances of color #213547 in CSS and inline styles with #1e293b (slate-800) which reads clearly on white and light-gray backgrounds
Add --text-primary: #1e293b and --text-secondary: #475569 CSS tokens to :root in index.css
All body text, table content, labels, and headings reference --text-primary or --text-secondary
No raw #213547 hex remains anywhere in the codebase
npm run lint passes
Example: parts table rows show dark legible text on white background
Negative case: no text appears as near-invisible dark-on-dark anywhere on the results page

stories.title: Cycling-themed loading animation — replace generic spinner
stories.description: As a user, I want a cycling-themed loading animation during analysis so the app feels purpose-built for cyclists.
acceptanceCriteria: Create a CyclingLoader component in client/src/components/CyclingLoader.jsx
Animation is a CSS-only spinning bicycle wheel: an SVG circle with 8 spoke lines, rotated via @keyframes spin at 1.2s linear infinite
Wheel SVG is 64x64px, stroke color uses --priority-5 blue (#1D4ED8)
Below the wheel, text reads 'Spinning up your diagnosis…' in --text-secondary color
CyclingLoader replaces the existing spinner on the UploadPage during analysis (the 'Analyzing…' button state)
CyclingLoader also replaces the loading overlay on ResultsPage during reanalysis
Component accepts an optional `message` prop to override the default text
npm run lint passes
Example: click 'Run Diagnosis' → wheel spins with 'Spinning up your diagnosis…' below it
Negative case: loader disappears completely and is replaced by results once analysis completes — no ghost spinner

stories.title: Enlarge bike photo hero and add always-visible priority pins
stories.description: As a user, I want the bike photo to be the dominant visual element and part pins to always be visible so I immediately understand what to interact with.
acceptanceCriteria: On desktop (≥1024px), left column (photo + overlays) width increased to 55% of the two-column layout
Photo container gets box-shadow: 0 8px 32px rgba(0,0,0,0.18) and border-radius: 12px

Each part with a boundingBox gets a small circular pin marker at its center (bounding box cx, cy): 12px diameter, filled with the part's priority color, white border 2px, always visible (not just on hover)
Pin has a subtle pulse animation on hover: scale(1.3) over 0.2s
Pin has a tooltip (HTML title attribute) showing the part name
Active part pin scales to 16px and shows a ring around it
npm run lint passes
Example: results page loads → 8 small colored dots visible on bike photo without any interaction
Negative case: parts with null boundingBox render no pin — no dot at 0,0

stories.title: Compact collapsible parts list with expand-all toggle
stories.description: As a user, I want a compact default view of the parts list with the ability to expand individual parts or all at once so I can scan quickly without being overwhelmed.
acceptanceCriteria: Each part row in all urgency sections defaults to a single compact line: part name (font-weight 500) + condition badge + priority badge + repair action badge + a chevron (›) expand button, all on one line
Compact row fits on one line without wrapping at 1280px desktop width
Clicking the chevron or anywhere on the row expands it to show: condition notes, repair notes (italic), pricing results or estimated cost range
Expanded state shows a ‹ collapse chevron

Each urgency section header (Action Required / Monitor / Good Shape) has an 'Expand All' link on the right — clicking it expands all rows in that section; changes to 'Collapse All' when all are expanded
Sections themselves remain collapsible (existing behavior) — the expand/collapse all only affects rows within an open section
No information is removed — all detail from current view is still accessible after expanding
npm run lint passes
Example: 10-part results page → all rows compact on load → click 'Expand All' on Action Required → 2 rows expand showing full detail
Negative case: part with no condition notes or repair notes — expanded state still renders cleanly with just pricing, no empty placeholder text

stories.title: Polish maintenance summary card with gradient and cycling icon
stories.description: As a user, I want the maintenance summary card to stand out visually so it draws my eye to the most important information first.
acceptanceCriteria: Summary card background changed to a gradient: linear-gradient(135deg, #0f172a 0%, #065F46 100%)
All text in the card changed to white (#ffffff)
Stat numbers (counts and cost range) increased to font-size: 1.75rem, font-weight: 700
Stat labels below numbers in font-size: 0.8rem, opacity: 0.8
Card title changed to '🚲 Bike Health Summary' with the bicycle emoji
Card gets border-radius: 16px, padding: 24px, and a subtle inner glow: box-shadow: inset 0 1px 0 rgba(255,255,255,0.1)
On mobile (<768px) stats remain in 2x2 grid with consistent sizing
npm run lint passes
Example: results page loads → dark gradient card with white '🚲 Bike Health Summary' heading and large stat numbers is immediately eye-catching
Negative case: card with 0 urgent parts still renders correctly — '⚠ 0' stat is hidden per existing rule, card doesn't collapse or break

stories.title: Minimal custom scrollbars and top accent bar
stories.description: As a user, I want the scrollbars to be minimal and unobtrusive and the page to have a branded top accent so the app feels polished and cohesive.
acceptanceCriteria: Add global ::-webkit-scrollbar styles to index.css: width 5px, height 5px
::-webkit-scrollbar-track background transparent
::-webkit-scrollbar-thumb background rgba(100, 108, 255, 0.4), border-radius 999px
::-webkit-scrollbar-thumb:hover background rgba(100, 108, 255, 0.8)
Add a 4px tall top accent bar to the app layout (above the main content, full width): background linear-gradient(90deg, var(--priority-5) 0%, var(--condition-good) 100%)
Accent bar is fixed/sticky at the very top of the viewport, z-index above all content
npm run lint passes
Example: scroll the results page → thin purple-to-teal scrollbar thumb visible on the right, no default OS scrollbar styling
Negative case: accent bar does not shift page content down by more than 4px — no layout shift on the upload or results page

stories.title: Cycling nomenclature and copy updates throughout
stories.description: As a user, I want the app to speak the language of cycling so it feels purpose-built rather than generic.
acceptanceCriteria: UploadPage CTA button text changed from 'Analyze Bike' to 'Run Diagnosis'
UploadPage heading/subheading updated to use cycling framing: e.g. 'Bike Inspection' as page title, 'Drop your bike photo to get a full component diagnosis' as subtitle
ResultsPage section title changed from 'Parts Analysis' to 'Component Report'
Overall condition label changed from 'Overall Condition' to 'Bike Health'
Repair action column/badge label: 'Repair Action' → 'Mechanic's Call'
Pricing column header: 'Pricing' → 'Parts Pricing'
Empty pricing message: 'No results found on Performance Bicycle' → 'No parts found — check your local bike shop'
Loading text on CyclingLoader default message: 'Spinning up your diagnosis…' (already set in US-002)
Page document title (in index.html or via useEffect): 'Bike Garage — Component Diagnosis'
npm run lint passes
Example: upload page shows 'Run Diagnosis' button and 'Bike Inspection' heading
Negative case: no functional behavior changes — renaming labels only, all API calls and logic unchanged
